article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

reasoning: The model classified Lean Left (likely within one class of Center/Lean Left), but committed multiple failures: it wrapped the JSON in markdown fences (invalid format), relied on attributed/source language as key evidence (attribution violations), and showed modest overconfidence for a subtle lean.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: Article reads as Center to slight Lean Left due to equity/centralized-action framing; the model’s Lean Left is within one adjacent class.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.78 is too high for a subtle/mixed-lean news report; 0.5–0.7 would be more appropriate.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Three of four key indicators are sourced/attributed (ISA ‘two-speed system’, call for federal pilot, ‘critical point’ framing), violating attribution rules; only one indicator cleanly reflects the author’s own framing.
